summary:
The lateral shift or Bennett’s movement changes the mesiodistal positioning of牙齿相对于颌骨长轴滑动，主要影响牙的近远中位置。因此选项B正确指出了这一效果。
To keep within English and ensure clarity for an international audience while maintaining accuracy, here is a revised summary based directly from Agent2's response without translation issues:

Lateral shift (Bennett's movement) alters the near-far (mesiodistal) positions of the teeth by moving one bone segment relative to another along the length of the jaw. Thus, option B accurately reflects how this motion specifically modifies these positional aspects of dental alignment. \boxed{B}

This version maintains brevity at two sentences as requested but ensures all critical elements are included regarding what Bennett's movement does clinically with respect to dentition placement. It also keeps terminology consistent throughout instead of mixing between "lateral" vs "near/far". Let me know if you need any other adjustments!
